verses.text: A Páscoa de Josias — a grande celebração nacional aqui registrada — foi a mais completa desde os dias de Samuel (v. 18). Após décadas de descuido, a festa foi guardada conforme o tempo da Torá, no décimo quarto dia do primeiro mês. O reavivamento na Escritura, muitas vezes, começa com o retorno a práticas esquecidas, guardadas no tempo certo e do modo certo.

Josias pôs os sacerdotes em seus turnos e os encorajou. O hebraico *chazaq* — “fortalecer, encorajar” — nomeia o que o rei fez com suas palavras. A reforma não consiste apenas em corrigir estruturas; é fortalecer as pessoas que servem dentro delas. O encorajamento de um líder pode reconstruir uma vocação.
A Arca sagrada pode ter sido removida do Templo durante a apostasia de Manassés ou Amom.

As atribuições dos sacerdotes e levitas refletiam as direções de Davi e Salomão ( **8.14-15** ; **1Cr 24-26** ). O animal da Páscoa era tipicamente abatido pelo ofertante ( **Dt 16.5-6** ). No entanto, como os ofertantes não tinham tempo para se purificar, Josias continuou a prática que Ezequias havia iniciado, fazendo com que os levitas abatessem os animais da Páscoa (veja **2Cr 30.13-20** ). No tempo de Josias, o grande número de participantes também poderia ter causado problemas logísticos.

No tempo de Josias, o grande número de participantes também poderia ter causado problemas logísticos.

O sacrifício da Páscoa exigia cordeiros e cabritos ( **Êx 12.21** ). O gado era uma oferta adicional. Os totais que Josias forneceu, juntamente com as contribuições de outros, foram quase o dobro das ofertas no tempo de Ezequias (veja **2Cr 30.24** ), mas menos do que as ofertas na dedicação do Templo (veja **7.5** ).

35:10 So the service was prepared, &c.—All the necessary preparations having been completed, and the appointed time having arrived for the passover, the solemnity was celebrated. One remarkable feature in the account is the prominent part that was taken by the Levites in the preparation of the sacrifices; namely, the killing and stripping of the skins, which were properly the peculiar duties of the priests; but as those functionaries were not able to overtake the extraordinary amount of work and the Levites had been duly sanctified for the service, they were enlisted for the time in this priestly employment.

35:11 They killed the passover - The people themselves might slay their own paschal lambs, and then present the blood to the priests, that they might sprinkle it before the altar; and the Levites flayed them, and made them ready for dressing.

35:12 they removed the burnt offerings—Some of the small cattle being designed for burnt offerings were put apart by themselves, that they might not be intermingled with the paschal lambs, which were carefully selected according to certain rules, and intended to be sacramentally eaten; and the manner in which those burnt offerings were presented seems to have been the following: "All the subdivisions of the different fathers' houses came one after another to the altar in solemn procession to bring to the priests the portions which had been cut off, and the priests laid these pieces upon the fire of the altar of burnt offering."
Os levitas trouxeram a comida rapidamente , observando o elemento de pressa exigido na Páscoa ( **Êx 12.11** ).

35:14 afterwards they made ready for themselves, and for the priests—The Levites rendered this aid to the priests solely because they were so engrossed the entire day that they had no leisure to provide any refreshments for themselves.

35:15 And the singers . . ., were in their place—While the priests and people were so much engaged, the choir was not idle. They had to sing certain Psalms, namely, the hundred thirteenth to the hundred eighteenth inclusive, once, twice, and even a third time, during the continuance of each company of offerers. As they could not leave their posts, for the singing was resumed as every fresh company entered, the Levites prepared for them also; for the various bands relieved each other in turn, and while the general choir was doing duty, a portion of the tuneful brethren, relieved for a time, partook of the viands that were brought them.

35:16 So all the service of the Lord was prepared the same day,.... With every sacrifice, and for all sorts of persons: to keep the passover, and to offer burnt offerings upon the altar of the Lord; which were required to be done on that day: according to the commandment of King Josiah; which was, that every thing be provided, prepared, and done, as the law required.

35:17 And the children of Israel that were present kept the passover at that time,.... In its proper time, on the fourteenth day of Nisan: and the feast of unleavened bread seven days; the seven days following the passover, as the Lord by Moses appointed.

Esta Páscoa contou com um número maior de participantes do que a que Ezequias havia realizado. Nesta Páscoa, os sacerdotes e levitas desempenharam um papel proeminente e adequado, conforme Josias havia especificamente exigido ( **35.15-16** ; cp. **30.3** , **15** ).

35:19 In the eighteenth year of the reign Josiah was this passover kept—"It is said (Kg2 22:3) that Josiah sent Shaphan to Hilkiah in the eighth month of that year." If this statement rests upon an historical basis, all the events narrated here (at 2Ch. 34:8-35:19) must have happened in about the space of five months and a half. We should then have a proof that the eighteenth year of Josiah's reign was reckoned from the autumn (compare 2 Chr 29:3).

A narrativa salta do décimo oitavo ano de Josias (622 a.C.) para o ano de sua morte (609 a.C.). O declínio do Império Assírio trouxe Egito e Babilônia, duas grandes potências que há muito tempo estavam sujeitas à Assíria, em conflito uma com a outra. O rei Neco do Egito , que havia se aliado à Assíria para resistir à expansão babilônica, pediu a Josias que permitisse a passagem livre de seu exército. A interceptação de Josias pode ter sido o resultado de uma coalizão com Babilônia, ou pode ter sido sua própria tentativa de estabelecer independência do Egito. A morte de Josias foi causada por sua desobediência a um oráculo divino entregue por um rei gentio.\n\n

Jeremias tinha Josias em alta estima (veja **Jr 22.15-16** ; cp. **Jr 22.10** ). O Livro das Lamentações foi perdido; não está relacionado ao Livro das Lamentações.

35:26 The rest of the acts of Josiah, and his goodness, according to that which was written in the law of the Lord: The closing formula for Josiah measures him by the very book his reign rediscovered. *Wahasadav* — his acts of covenant-faithfulness — are evaluated by the standard he himself reintroduced. No higher epitaph could be written.

35:27 And his deeds, first and last - "The former things which he did in his childhood, and the latter things which he did in his youth; and all the judgments which he pronounced from his eighth year, when he came to the kingdom, to his eighteenth, when he was grown up, and began to repair the sanctuary of the Lord; and all that he brought of his substance to the hand of judgment, purging both the house of Israel and Judah from all uncleanness;
